title: MAGI etc contemporary tech references in Eva
display_title: MAGI etc contemporary tech references in Eva
wikitext:
[]: MAGI, etc. -- contemporary tech references in Eva             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Sat Jun 21, 2008 6:37 pm Call it a nutty hypothesis, but I draw a lot of connections between the description of the MAGI system and another, similar project by the Japanese government in effect at the time -- the Fifth Generation Computer Project (or as I like to call it, Project 5G) . Project 5G was well known in Japan at the time, and worldwide before that, and it got cancelled shortly after Eva aired. Let's have the run down: [code:1] Parallelization: MAGI & 5G Predicate logic: 5G and (likely) MAGI Fuzzy logic competition between knowledge bases: MAGI & (proposed) 5G Big, custom architecture: MAGI & 5G Expert system usage: MAGI & 5G Absurd speed and complexity: MAGI & 5G Government funding: MAGI & 5G Run time monitors: MAGI & 5G Manned by full-time staff of white coated scientist types: MAGI & 5G Out of place neural nets: MAGI [/code:1] Not bad, eh? Also, project 5G was aiming for the same sorts of things -- large scale parallelization and fast data processing combined with an (albiet entirely logical) human-level intelligence, and capable of running things without humans after an apocalyptic event. Though this project itself dissolved (mainly due to really crappy design, inflexible technologies, economic naiievete, and people screwing around with the prolog language design without understanding what makes it work as well as it does), it paved the way for absurd levels of funding for similar (more successful) projects both in Japan and abroad -- including facial recognition, bipedal movement, realistic facial expressions, and really effective speech recognition/parsing. Lots of the concepts used in Asimo, PaPeRo, and the (american) Phillip K Dick replicant were originated in projects funded either by the same organization as 5G or to compete with said organization. Do you guys agree or disagree that the MAGI system was meant as a reference, and why?

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sat Jun 21, 2008 6:42 pm there've been all sorts of projects like this throughout the years. It doesn't stop my Laptop from being more powerful than anything they could possibly have ever built. Which is why I completely disagree. It was just a big budget money throwaway and some interesting theories. The fact is, computers work on an entirely different principal to the human brain and can never be truly intelligent.

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Sat Jun 21, 2008 7:06 pm No. I am talking about the creators making a reference to 5G in their descriptions of the MAGI system. Basically, every majorly-popularized point about 5G was stuck in the descriptions, merged into the existing "big computer" trope. Now, from what I understand, at the time in Japan, the morning news would give dumbed-down descriptions of new advancements in project 5G technology the same way that in 1999 in the US the news would give dumbed down descriptions of "new" computer technologies like high speed internet and faster chips, without really understanding even the dumbed down descriptions. Ornette mentioned something on the IRC as well: Ornette wrote: [17:57:27] &Ornette | Misato: Yes, it's a system that implants an individual's personality into a seventh generation organic computer so it can think.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jun 22, 2008 12:23 am The fact is, computers work on an entirely different principal to the human brain and can never be truly intelligent.

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Sun Jun 22, 2008 9:26 am AuraTwilight wrote: The fact is, computers work on an entirely different principal to the human brain and can never be truly intelligent.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sun Jun 22, 2008 9:33 am My $.02 worth: While computers can be remarkably intelligent, they are also remarkably stupid in that they can't implement decisions on their own--they still need a human being to actually do that. Sure, they can calculate the best course of action, but human beings are the ones who will make the decision to follow it.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Jun 22, 2008 10:36 am i disagree until you provide more information on 5G from an outside source or a comment from someone on NGE's production team. Its an interesting idea, but the idea of 3 powerful supercomputers running together to control and run a city nearly autonomously never struck me as particularly unique. Consider the computer systems that run the space shuttle. IIRC, there are three computers programmed independently that must agree before automatic course corrections or other actions are carried out. Sure there may be some similarities between the MAGI and 5G, but to say that the MAGI is a direct reference to 5G is quite extreme and requires much more in the way of evidence.

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Sun Jun 22, 2008 10:37 am Sharaz Destler wrote: My $.02 worth: While computers can be remarkably intelligent, they are also remarkably stupid in that they can't implement decisions on their own--they still need a human being to actually do that. Sure, they can calculate the best course of action, but human beings are the ones who will make the decision to follow it.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Jun 22, 2008 10:37 am The idea of supercomputers controlling a city/country/world isn't a very new idea (even in '95). It'd been a sci-fi staple for quite a while, even Star Trek in the 60's used the idea once or twice.

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Sun Jun 22, 2008 10:44 am slothen wrote: i disagree until you provide more information on 5G from an outside source or a comment from someone on NGE's production team. Its an interesting idea, but the idea of 3 powerful supercomputers running together to control and run a city nearly autonomously never struck me as particularly unique. Consider the computer systems that run the space shuttle. IIRC, there are three computers programmed independently that must agree before automatic course corrections or other actions are carried out. Sure there may be some similarities between the MAGI and 5G, but to say that the MAGI is a direct reference to 5G is quite extreme and requires much more in the way of evidence.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Jun 22, 2008 10:50 am Enki v.2 wrote: For more info, click the link in the first post.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Mon Jun 23, 2008 10:59 am Enki v.2 wrote: Thanks for ignoring the topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Mon Jun 23, 2008 3:38 pm I'm comparing features and goals, not levels of supposed ability. Arguing about whether or not the magi could do all they do in Eva in real life isn't part of the topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jun 23, 2008 3:58 pm Please, take 10 and breathe deeply before pressing the Submit button. (But don't hyperventilate, as we can't guarantee to send Kaworu around in time to assist with some mouth to mouth. ) There are a number of sub topics here -- strong AI/pre-Singularity AI development/Anno's primitivist manifesto/infrastructure redundancy strategies... Some of them fit this sub-forum, some go in off-topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Mon Jun 23, 2008 4:19 pm Good point. Perhaps this should be restricted to just how it relates to the MAGI system in the show?

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Wed Jun 25, 2008 4:09 pm The question here is: ARE THE MAGI A REFERENCE TO A REAL LIFE Japanese COMPUTER PROJECT? I belive the answer is yes.

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Wed Jun 25, 2008 4:58 pm universalperson wrote: The question here is: ARE THE MAGI A REFERENCE TO A REAL LIFE Japanese COMPUTER PROJECT? I belive the answer is yes.
